text:
{
  "title": "Ordinal self-report health polarity",
  "group_name": "self_report_health_polarity",
  "family": "ordinal_categorical_composite",
  "summary": "Represent each student's subjective lifestyle reports as a compact polarity profile that captures whether their stated habits consistently lean protective, adverse, neutral, or internally mixed.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Use only categorical lifestyle fields with natural health direction and exclude gender. Map stress_level as low=1, medium=0, high=-1; sleep_quality as good=1, average=0, poor=-1; physical_activity_level as active=1, moderate=0, sedentary=-1; smoking_alcohol as no=1, occasional=0, yes=-1; diet_type as balanced=1, veg=0, non-veg=-1. For each row, ignore missing values rather than treating them as neutral, then derive signed_sum, signed_mean over observed mapped fields, protective_count, adverse_count, neutral_count, observed_count, consensus_margin=abs(protective_count-adverse_count)/observed_count, mixed_polarity_count=min(protective_count,adverse_count), and all_protective/all_adverse flags when observed_count>=3 and all observed nonneutral signs are on one side. If observed_count is 0, set ratios and signed_mean to 0 and all flags to 0. This follows ordered-categorical representation ideas from https://dotdata.com/blog/feature-engineering-for-categorical-attributes/ and domain evidence linking college-student sleep, stress, and activity patterns from https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/public-health/articles/10.3389/fpubh.2025.1640974/full and https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2025/25_0118.htm.",
  "expected_signal": "The target may separate rare fit and unhealthy rows partly through consistent stacks of self-reported protective or adverse behaviors, so compact polarity and consensus features can expose class-balanced signal that is diluted when each category is considered independently.",
  "risk": "The imposed ordering is partly heuristic, especially for diet_type and occasional smoking_alcohol, and the group may be redundant with raw categorical columns or broad lifestyle-burden features if the labels depend more on numeric physiology than reported habits."
}
